financial.note: BBL is the main financier for TCC Group's acquisition-led growth; BJC's high Net D/E ratio (~1.18x) makes interest expense highly sensitive to BBL's prime lending rate.
Holds approx. 1.5% equity; provides institutional liquidity and reflects sovereign-linked fund sentiment.

supply_chain.note: A 2025/2026 JV to optimize Big C's distribution and enter the healthcare distribution market; efficiency gains directly impact SG&A margins.
Supplies natural gas for glass furnace operations; energy is a primary cost driver in the Packaging Supply Chain segment.
Captive demand from TCC Group's beverage arm for glass containers and aluminum cans; performance of ThaiBev beverages drives BJC's packaging volume.

competitors.note: Direct competition between Big C and Lotus's/Makro in hypermarket and wholesale segments.
Mini Big C competes for convenience market share against 7-Eleven's dominant footprint.
Competition in the consumer packaging and paper-based product segments.
Primary rival in the domestic glass container manufacturing market.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Major raw material for can manufacturing; rising global aluminum prices compress margins in the Packaging segment.
Direct cost input for tissue brands (Cellox, Zilk); price spikes negatively affect Consumer Supply Chain GPM.
BJC carries substantial debt (~200bn THB); a 25bps hike significantly increases finance costs and reduces net earnings.
Correlates strongly with Same Store Sales Growth (SSSG) for Big C's retail formats.
